name: Lemon Meringue Angel Food Cake
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces Unsalted butter (6 tablespoons), chilled and cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place fresh, cold egg whites and cold water in the bowl of a heavy-duty mixer. Set aside until they reach 60 degrees or slightly below room temperature.
2. Pour flour, 1/2 cup granulated sugar, and salt into a triple sifter or sieve. Sift onto a sheet of waxed paper; set aside.
3. Adjust rack to the lower third of the oven and preheat to 325 degrees. Set nearby a dry, ungreased 10-inch tube pan. A tube pan allows the batter to cling to the sides and rise to its full height.
4. Whip egg whites with a whisk attachment until frothy. Add cream of tartar and whip on medium speed until soft peaks form. Continue whipping and gradually add the 1 cup granulated sugar until the whites have thickened and form soft, droopy white peaks.
5. Add vanilla extract and lemon zest in the final moments of whipping.
6. Sprinkle one-quarter of the flour mixture over the whites, and with a rubber spatula, fold into the whites. Repeat this process with the remaining flour mixture, folding only one-quarter at a time.
7. Gently pour the batter into an ungreased tube pan. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes or until the top is light golden, the cake feels spongy and springs back when touched, and a wooden toothpick inserted in the middle comes out clean.
8. Invert the cake onto the neck of a bottle or funnel. Cool completely before removing from the pan.
9. To remove the cake from the pan, slip the tip of a small metal spatula between the cake and pan. Slowly trace the perimeter to release any cake sticking to the pan. Tilt the cake pan on its side and gently tap the bottom against the counter to loosen the cake. Rotate the cake pan, tapping as you turn it a few more times until it appears free.
10. Cover the cake with a rack or cardboard round and invert it onto the work surface at the same time. Tap firmly onto the surface to release any remaining air pockets.
11. Yield: One 10-inch round cake
For the Filling:
1. In a 1-1/2 quart heavy-bottomed saucepan, whisk together eggs, egg yolks, sugar, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Add butter and place over medium heat, stirring constantly until the mixture thickens.
2. Cook without boiling until it develops body and thickens. Remove from heat and pour through a stainless steel sieve into a bowl.
3. Cover with plastic wrap, making small slits for steam to escape while cooling. Refrigerate until chilled.
For the Frosting:
1. In a 3-cup saucepan, combine water and 2/3 cup sugar. Bring to a boil over low heat, dissolving the sugar.
2. Increase heat to medium high and continue boiling until a candy thermometer reads 235 degrees.
3. Whisk egg whites with cream of tartar until soft peaks form. Add 2 tablespoons sugar and continue whipping until stiff but not dry.
4. With mixer running, pour syrup onto whipped whites. Continue whipping on medium speed for about 3 more minutes.
5. Mixture will thicken, cool, and form glossy, stiff peaks. Add vanilla extract and frost the cake immediately.
Assemble: Cut cake into three layers. Spread filling evenly between layers, top with remaining layer, and frost the top and sides of the cake.
